query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Exhort', 'To urge, advise, or caution earnestly, admonish urgently', 'Scholars noted a striking exhort between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exhume', 'To dig out of the ground, to take out of a place of burial', 'The author hoped the new policy would exhume tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exigency', 'Exigent  state or character, urgency', 'Scholars noted a striking exigency between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exigent', 'Urgent, needing immediate action', 'What seemed exigent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Existence', 'The state of being, existing, or occurring, beinghood', 'Scholars noted a striking existence between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Existential', 'Of, or relating to existence', 'The author hoped the new policy would existential tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exodus', 'A sudden departure of a large number of people', 'The essay highlights the exodus of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exonerate', 'To officially absolve someone of blame', 'Over time, the community learned to exonerate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exorbitance', 'Exceeding the bounds of custom, propriety, or reason', 'Her exorbitance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exorbitant', 'Unreasonably high or excessive', '“The hotel charged an exorbitant fee for parking.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exorcise', 'To drive out an evil spirit from a person, place or thing', 'Critics argued that the plan might exorcise the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exorcism', 'The ceremony or the formula used in exorcising', 'Her exorcism became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exotic', 'Foreign, with the connotation of excitingly foreign', 'Scholars noted a striking exotic between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expand', 'To change from a smaller size to a larger one', 'Critics argued that the plan might expand the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expanse', 'A wide stretch, usually of sea, sky, or land', 'Her expanse became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expansion', 'The act or process of expanding', 'Scholars noted a striking expansion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expatiate', 'To range at large, or without restraint', 'The author hoped the new policy would expatiate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expatriate', 'To banish (a person) from his or her native country', 'The author hoped the new policy would expatriate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expectancy', 'Anticipation, the state of expecting something', 'Scholars noted a striking expectancy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expectorate', 'To cough up fluid from the lungs', 'Critics argued that the plan might expectorate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expediency', 'Suitability for particular circumstance or situation', 'Scholars noted a striking expediency between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expedient', 'Simple, easy, or quick, convenient', 'The passage presents a expedient portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expedite', 'To speed up a process', '“We need to expedite shipping for urgent orders.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expeditious', 'Done quickly and efficiently', 'The passage presents a expeditious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expend', 'To consume or exhaust some resource', 'Over time, the community learned to expend its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expiate', 'To atone for guilt or sin', 'The author hoped the new policy would expiate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Explicate', 'To explain meticulously or in great detail, to analyze', 'The author hoped the new policy would explicate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Explicit', 'Stated clearly and in detail', 'The passage presents a explicit portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exploit', 'Making use of a situation or resource in a way considered unfair or unethical', 'Her exploit became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exponent', 'One who expounds, represents or advocates', 'Scholars noted a striking exponent between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exposition', 'The act of expounding, setting forth, or explaining', 'The essay highlights the exposition of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expository', 'Serving to explain, explicate, or elucidate', 'Over time, the community learned to expository its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expostulate', 'To protest or remonstrate', 'Over time, the community learned to expostulate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exposure', 'The condition of being exposed, uncovered, or unprotected', 'Scholars noted a striking exposure between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expound', 'To set forth or state in detail', 'The essay highlights the expound of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expressive', 'Effectively conveying thought or feeling', 'The reviewer described the tone as expressive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expulsion', 'The act of driving out or expelling', 'The essay highlights the expulsion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expunge', 'To erase or remove completely', 'Over time, the community learned to expunge its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Expurgate', 'To edit out incorrect, or otherwise undesirable information', 'Critics argued that the plan might expurgate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extant', 'In existence, still existing, not destroyed or lost', 'The essay highlights the extant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extemporaneous', 'Without preparation or advanced thought, offhand', 'The reviewer described the tone as extemporaneous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extempore', 'Carried out with no preparation, impromptu', 'Scholars noted a striking extempore between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extensible', 'Capable of being extended', 'Her extensible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extensive', 'Covering a large area or amount', '“The library has an extensive collection of rare books.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extensor', 'A muscle whose contraction extends or straightens a limb', 'Her extensor became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extenuate', 'To represent (a fault, offense, etc ) as less serious', 'The author hoped the new policy would extenuate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('External', 'Outside of something', 'Her external became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extinct', 'No longer in existence, that has ended or died out', 'Scholars noted a striking extinct between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extinguish', 'To put out, as in fire, to end burning, to quench', 'The author hoped the new policy would extinguish tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extirpate', 'To remove or destroy totally, do away with, exterminate', 'The author hoped the new policy would extirpate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
